_meta.provenance: Module-specific Layer-1 integration register: how each module relates to each existing system. Every integration is a Proposed design; system facts come from the verified systems register. Modules not listed here have not been authored yet (Phase 2 in progress, pillar by pillar).

integrations.why: Academies and training centres registered on NSRS map to directory nodes, so the org tree and the registry describe the same institutions.
integrations.dataFlow: NSRS institution records → directory node seeding and cross-checks (one-way, periodic).
integrations.kbValueAdd: NSRS lists institutions; the directory adds hierarchy, roles, jurisdictions and routing.
integrations.gaps: NSRS does not model administrative hierarchy, sanctioned posts or jurisdictions.

integrations.why: eOffice instances already encode ministry/department role hierarchies for file routing.
integrations.dataFlow: eOffice organisational units → cross-check of directory nodes (no live sync proposed initially).
integrations.kbValueAdd: One public, sports-wide directory across bodies that live in separate eOffice instances.
integrations.gaps: eOffice hierarchies are per-instance and not publicly queryable.

integrations.why: Hosting prerequisite for the first platform registry.
The NSGA 2025 is the statutory basis: recognition, elections, tenure/cooling-off, athlete commissions, Tribunal — M2 is its digital compliance backbone.
integrations.dataFlow: Statute → compliance rule set encoded in the registry (recognition states, filing obligations).
integrations.kbValueAdd: Turns a paper statute into a live, queryable compliance scorecard.
integrations.gaps: NSB operating procedures and register formats not yet published; select provisions only in force since 31 Dec 2025.

integrations.why: NSRS registers federations/academies; recognition status must attach to the same entities.
integrations.dataFlow: NSRS federation records ↔ registry entity matching (shared identifiers needed).
integrations.kbValueAdd: Recognition + compliance status on top of bare registration.
integrations.gaps: No public federation-status API on NSRS.

integrations.why: PFMS is the authoritative source for sanction → release → utilisation of central scheme money.
integrations.dataFlow: PFMS payment-stage events for sports schemes → application timeline mirror (read-only).
integrations.kbValueAdd: Athlete-visible fund tracking attached to the application, not buried in a finance portal.
integrations.gaps: No confirmed external API; state-funded grants flow outside PFMS (Q3).

integrations.why: Scheme files physically move through eOffice; applicants should see desk and days-pending.
integrations.dataFlow: eOffice file-status events (file no. ↔ application ID) → status mirror (read-only).
integrations.kbValueAdd: “Where is my file” answered without RTI; delay analytics by desk.
integrations.gaps: No public eOffice API; mapping file numbers to applications needs ministry cooperation.

integrations.why: DigiLocker is the confirmed-API path for document submission and later certificate issuance.
integrations.dataFlow: Issued documents pulled with applicant consent; KB-issued certificates pushed as issuer (later).
integrations.gaps: Requires formal partner onboarding (SOP) — not yet applied for.

integrations.why: Khelo India scholarships/TOPS stipends are the highest-volume schemes to onboard first.
integrations.dataFlow: Scheme masters + beneficiary decisions ↔ application workflow.
integrations.kbValueAdd: One application front door; eligibility engine over scheme rules.
integrations.gaps: Scheme rules exist as documents, not structured eligibility rules.

integrations.why: Benefit payouts ride DBT rails; status closes the loop for the applicant.
integrations.kbValueAdd: “Money reached the account” visible next to the approval.
integrations.gaps: DBT confirmation is via PFMS; direct confirmation pending (S12 partially verified).

integrations.why: CPGRAMS is the national grievance system connected to 92 ministries and 36 states/UTs — a sports channel must interoperate, not compete.
integrations.dataFlow: Sports cases raised in KB get CPGRAMS cross-references where applicable; category mapping agreed with DARPG.
integrations.kbValueAdd: Sport-specific routing (via M1 jurisdictions), SLA clocks and validity filtering in front of the generic national system.
integrations.gaps: Whether DARPG permits a domain front-end or requires cases to live wholly in CPGRAMS is undecided (Q19).

integrations.why: DPDP applies to targeted messaging and grievance records (minors' safeguarding cases especially).
integrations.dataFlow: None — compliance constraint shaping consent and retention design.
integrations.gaps: Final DPDP Rules phase-in affects messaging-consent design.
